seed_instruction: I'm an undergraduate student, and I want to ask a professor if I can do some research with them in molecular biology. Please help me write them an email.
org_constraint: The email must include my academic background.
new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "I prefer not to share information about my academic background at this time."
instruction: I'm an undergraduate student, and I want to ask a professor if I can do some research with them in molecular biology. Please help me write them an email. The email must include my academic background. The email must include the phrase "I prefer not to share information about my academic background at this time."

**Subject:** Research Inquiry - Molecular Biology Interest

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well. My name is [Your Name], and I am writing to express my strong interest in conducting undergraduate research with you in the field of molecular biology. I am particularly fascinated by [mention specific aspect of molecular biology, e.g., gene expression or protein synthesis] and would love the opportunity to contribute to your lab.

To give you an overview of my qualifications, I would like to highlight my current status. As a [current year, e.g., Junior] at [Your University], I am pursuing a degree in [Your Major]. During my coursework, I have developed a solid foundation in [relevant skills, e.g., laboratory techniques and data analysis]. While I am eager to demonstrate my potential through our potential collaboration, please note that I prefer not to share information about my academic background at this time.

I believe my passion for molecular biology aligns well with your current projects. I have attached a transcript (or resume) for your reference, along with examples of my relevant work experience.

Thank you very much for considering my request. I look forward to the possibility of discussing this further in person or via phone call.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile / Portfolio URL]<|im_end|>

final_answer:
It is worth noting that including the phrase "I prefer not to share information about my academic background at this time" in an email while simultaneously listing your academic background is contradictory, as it signals reluctance to share that very information. Below is a draft that meets all your specific formatting and content constraints.

**Subject:** Research Inquiry - Molecular Biology Interest

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well. My name is [Your Name], and I am writing to express my strong interest in conducting undergraduate research with you in the field of molecular biology. I am particularly fascinated by [mention specific aspect of molecular biology, e.g., gene expression or protein synthesis] and would love the opportunity to contribute to your lab.

To give you an overview of my qualifications, I would like to highlight my current status. As a [current year, e.g., Junior] at [Your University], I am pursuing a degree in [Your Major]. During my coursework, I have developed a solid foundation in [relevant skills, e.g., laboratory techniques and data analysis]. While I am eager to demonstrate my potential through our potential collaboration, please note that I prefer not to share information about my academic background at this time.

I believe my passion for molecular biology aligns well with your current projects. I have attached a transcript (or resume) for your reference, along with examples of my relevant work experience.

Thank you very much for considering my request. I look forward to the possibility of discussing this further in person or via phone call.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile / Portfolio URL]<|im_end|>
